rule.title: New rule — basics
rule.description: Name the rule, pick the target entities to enforce against, and choose the rule mode.

data_description.rule_name: Display name shown in the UI. Must be unique.
data_description.target_entities: One or more entities this rule will enforce.
data_description.mode: State mode forces a specific state. Attribute mode clamps numeric attributes or matches a light color target.

state.title: State mode
state.description: When any target entity enters one of the trigger states, force it back to the target state after the optional delay.

data_description.trigger_states: List of states that arm enforcement (e.g. on, open, unlocked). Type to add custom values.
data_description.target_state: State the entity will be forced to (e.g. off, closed, locked).
data_description.delay_seconds: Wait this long after a trigger before enforcing. 0 = immediate. Max 86400 (24h).

attribute.title: Attribute mode
attribute.description: For numeric attributes, when the value crosses the threshold, clamp it back to the target value after the optional delay. For light color attributes, enforce the target color whenever the current color differs.

data_description.attribute: Attribute to monitor. Color options are available only for light entities.
data_description.operator: Comparison operator used for numeric clamp rules.
data_description.threshold: Numeric value compared against the live attribute. Only used for numeric clamp rules.
data_description.target_value: Value to enforce. Numeric attributes clamp to this value; rgb_color and color_temp_kelvin must match it.
data_description.delay_seconds: Wait this long after a trigger before enforcing. 0 = immediate. Max 86400 (24h).

extras.title: Optional features
extras.description: Tick any features you want to configure. Each ticked option adds a follow-up screen. Skip them all to go straight to review.

data_description.add_flags: Tick to require extra conditions (e.g. only enforce when nobody is home, only after sunset). Leave unticked to enforce every time the trigger fires.
data_description.custom_rate_limit: Loop protection auto-suppresses the rule if it fires more than 10 times per minute (default). Leave unticked to use the default. Tick to set your own number, or to turn loop protection off entirely on a later screen.
data_description.add_debounce: Tick to ignore quick back-and-forth changes. The rule only acts if the trigger stays the same for the time you set on a later screen. Leave unticked to act on every change.

flags.title: Conditions (optional)
flags.description:
Extra conditions that must all be true for the rule to act.

Examples:
  • person.alice = not_home — only when Alice is away
  • input_boolean.guest_mode = off — only when no guests
  • sun.sun = below_horizon — only after sunset
  • binary_sensor.work_hours = on — only during work hours

To add a condition: pick an entity, type the state it must be in, then click Submit.

If you don't want any condition right now, leave both fields empty and click Submit to skip.

Conditions added so far ({count}): {summary}

Tick 'Add another' to chain more — all must match for the rule to fire.

data.add_another: Add another condition after this one

data_description.entity: Pick the entity whose state acts as a condition (e.g. person.alice, input_boolean.guest_mode, sun.sun).
data_description.match_state: The state the entity must be in for the rule to fire (e.g. 'on', 'home', 'not_home', 'below_horizon').
data_description.add_another: Tick to add more conditions. Leave unticked to finish.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Set the options you opted into on the previous screen. The default rate limit is {default_rate} enforcements per minute.

data_description.debounce_seconds: Window in seconds. Min 0, max 86400 (24h). Only shown when debounce was enabled on the previous step.
data_description.rate_limit_enabled: Tick to keep loop protection on (recommended). Untick to disable it for this rule — runaway loops will not be stopped automatically.
data_description.max_enforcements_per_minute: How many enforcements per minute are allowed before the rule auto-suppresses for 15 minutes. Min 1, max 600. Ignored when loop protection is unticked above.

safety.title: Safety acknowledgment
safety.description: This rule targets safety-sensitive entities ({entities}). You must explicitly acknowledge the risk to continue.

data.safety_acknowledged: I understand this rule will move physical hardware and accept the risk.

preview.title: Review and create

error.empty_target_entities: Pick at least one target entity.
error.empty_trigger_states: Provide at least one trigger state.
error.invalid_threshold: Threshold and target value must be numeric.
error.invalid_delay: Delay must be a whole number between 0 and 86400 seconds (24h).
error.invalid_rate: Max enforcements per minute must be a whole number between 1 and 600.
error.target_in_triggers: Target state cannot also be a trigger state — that would create an enforcement loop.
error.forbidden_state: States 'unavailable', 'unknown', and 'none' cannot be used as triggers or targets.
error.incomplete_flag: Both flag entity and required state must be set.
error.safety_not_acknowledged: You must acknowledge the safety risk to continue.
error.name_already_exists: A rule with this name already exists.
error.invalid_rgb_color: RGB color must be a list of three values between 0 and 255.
error.invalid_color_temp_kelvin: Color temperature must be between 2000 K and 6500 K.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Entity Guard hub can be configured.
abort.name_already_exists: A rule with this name already exists.

edit_safety.title: Safety acknowledgment
edit_safety.description: New safety-sensitive entities were added. Re-acknowledge to save.

data.safety_acknowledged: I understand this rule will move physical hardware and accept the risk.

edit_flags.title: Edit flag conditions
edit_flags.description: Current flags: {summary}. Choose 'add' to append, 'replace' to replace all, 'clear' to remove all, or 'save' to leave unchanged.

abort.name_already_exists: A rule with this name already exists.
abort.hub_no_options: The Entity Guard hub has no editable options.

rule_not_found.message: No Entity Guard rule found matching {rule_id}.

suppress.name: Suppress rule
suppress.description: Temporarily suppress an Entity Guard rule. The rule auto-resumes after the duration.

rule_id.name: Rule
rule_id.description: Rule UUID or rule name (config entry title).

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long to suppress the rule (1-1440 minutes).

unsuppress.name: Unsuppress rule
unsuppress.description: Resume a suppressed Entity Guard rule immediately.

rule_id.name: Rule
rule_id.description: Rule UUID or rule name (config entry title).

list_rules.name: List rules
list_rules.description: Return the configuration and current status of every Entity Guard rule.

clear_history.name: Clear rule history
clear_history.description: Clear stored enforcement history (counts, last enforced, cooldowns) for a rule.

rule_id.name: Rule
rule_id.description: Rule UUID or rule name (config entry title).

panic_stop.name: Panic stop
panic_stop.description: Emergency kill — disable every rule, clear all cooldowns, suppress for 60 minutes, and turn off the hub master switch.

flag_entity_missing.title: Flag entity missing in rule "{rule_name}"
flag_entity_missing.description: The following flag entity/entities no longer exist but are referenced in the rule: {missing_entities}. The rule will not enforce when these flags cannot be evaluated. Update or remove the rule to resolve this issue.
